top_stories.title: Metro Pacific Water tapped as interim Bulacan operator
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines – Metro Pacific Water (MPW) has been tapped by the city government of San Jose del Monte, Bulacan, as the interim operator of its water distribution operations. The local government signed the operations and maintenance deal on May 20 after securing authority to appoint a temporary partner to sta...
top_stories.why_it_matters: This belongs in the top group because its effects reach across multiple parts of the technology ecosystem, such as businesses, governments, infrastructure operators, consumers, or regulators.

top_stories.title: But rate hike remains on the table – economists
top_stories.summary: The softer-than-expected inflation print in May may give the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas room to take a more measured approach to tightening, although economists said another rate hike remains likely as underlying price pressures persist.

top_stories.title: Philippine inflation eases to 6.8% in May amid pump price rollbacks
top_stories.summary: By Katherine K. Chan, Reporter HEADLINE inflation came in slower than expected in May as pressures from transport costs eased following several pump price rollbacks during the month, the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) reported. Inflation settled at 6.8% in May, easing from 7.2% in April but quickened from the...

top_stories.title: CAMPI sees 5-8% drop in vehicle sales this year
top_stories.summary: THE CHAMBER of Automotive Manufacturers of the Philippines, Inc. (CAMPI) expects a smaller decline in vehicle sales this year than initially forecast, citing a month-on-month increase in car sales in May and recent fuel price rollbacks. “Three weeks ago, we said [total vehicle sales would be] around 8% to 10% lower...

top_stories.title: Diesel prices seen climbing next week on Middle East uncertainty
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines – Diesel prices could rise anew by June 9 amid fears on growing uncertainty in the Middle East peace deal.
